_meta.keyKnowledge: We explore the diversity of artworks by Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Peoples and consider culturally responsive approaches.

_source.w: We explore the diversity of artworks by Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Peoples and consider culturally responsive approaches.
y: Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander visual arts encompass extraordinary diversity across nations, regions and art forms.
Culturally responsive engagement ensures we approach First Nations art with the respect and understanding it deserves.
Understanding these art traditions challenges stereotypes and reveals the depth of the world's oldest living cultures.
eg: We explore the diversity of art practices across different Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander communities.
We learn about cultural protocols for viewing, discussing and responding to First Nations art.
We investigate how contemporary First Nations artists connect traditional practices with modern forms.
We discuss issues of cultural ownership, appropriation and respectful engagement.
We explore how art connects to Country, kinship, ceremony and cultural responsibility.
We engage with art from community art centres and First Nations galleries.
